ocr.text: T0 Mean 짹 Sd  T1 Mean 짹 Sd  T2 Mean 짹 Sd  T3 Mean 짹 Sd  T4 Mean 짹 Sd  Fried-man-Test  Wilcoxon-Test (T1-T4)  Effectsize(T1-T4)
SETK
SON-R (ref-age)
The following table presents the results of the statistical analyses conducted on the SETK assessments at various time points,
specifically from T0 to T4. The data reflects mean scores along with standard deviations (Sd) for each time interval, allowing
for a comprehensive understanding of the trends in performance over the observed periods. Notably, the T0 Mean 짹 Sd serves
as the baseline for comparison, while subsequent time points, T1 through T4, highlight the evolution of participants' scores as

interventions were implemented. 

The Friedman Test results are also included, providing insight into the overall differences
between the time points, while the Wilcoxon Test (T1-T4) specifically illustrates the significance of changes from T1 to T4.
This latter analysis is particularly critical for evaluating the effectiveness of the interventions applied during the study.
Furthermore, the Effect Size (T1-T4) metric offers a quantitative measure of the magnitude of observed changes, facilitating a
deeper understanding of the practical significance of the results. Together, these metrics contribute to a well-rounded assessment
of the SETK outcomes, supporting informed conclusions regarding the intervention's efficacy over time.
